skill.name: image-to-3d
skill.description: Image-to-3D asset creation for agents through Image Skill's zero-setup hosted runtime. Use when an input image should become a durable hosted 3D mesh asset, such as a glb, without provider credentials, OAuth, local runtime, or per-provider billing. Inspect the 3D model, pass one owned input image to edit, then keep jobs, asset URLs, receipts, JSON recovery, payments, and feedback in one loop.
skill.summary: Convert a single input image into a durable, hosted 3D mesh asset (glb) via a zero-setup CLI without provider credentials or local runtime.
skill.author: danielgwilson

security_audit.summary: Static analysis flagged 32 potential issues, all confirmed false positives after contextual review. The 'backtick execution' findings refer to markdown code-block formatting, not actual shell execution. Hardcoded URLs all point to the skill's own documented hosted contract (image-skill.com, api.image-skill.com). Hidden file references are standard $HOME and $CODEX_HOME environment variable expansions for workspace configuration. No malicious code, no credential exfiltration, and no obfuscation patterns were found. The skill legitimately invokes the published image-skill CLI via npx and accesses the filesystem only to set install paths.

low_findings.title: External CLI Invocation via npx
low_findings.description: The skill instructs agents to run `npx -y image-skill@latest` to execute a third-party CLI. This is the documented primary workflow and the only mechanism for performing the image-to-3D conversion. Risk is inherent to the stated purpose.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The npx invocation is the explicitly documented, intended mechanism. No obfuscation, no dynamic download of unverified code, and the package is pinned via @latest from a known publisher.

low_findings.title: Filesystem Path Configuration
low_findings.description: The skill sets $HOME and $CODEX_HOME environment variables to configure agent skill installation paths. This is standard workspace configuration, not hidden file access.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: These are standard shell environment variable expansions documented in the install instructions. No access to sensitive hidden files or system directories.

content.value_statement: Turning a single product photo into a usable 3D mesh usually requires provider accounts, API keys, and local GPU setup. This skill removes that friction by routing the conversion through a hosted runtime that returns a durable glb asset URL.
actual_capabilities: Convert a single input image into a textured 3D mesh (glb format) using the fal.trellis-image-to-3d model
Run a zero-setup guide command to discover required inputs and model parameters before spending credits
Execute an edit command that produces a hosted, durable asset URL for the generated 3D model
Set a per-image cost ceiling via the --max-estimated-usd-per-image flag to prevent overspend
Return machine-readable JSON output with jobs, asset URLs, receipts, and recovery data for downstream agent workflows
limitations: Currently uses only the fal.trellis-image-to-3d model; other 3D generation models are not yet wired into this intent skill
Requires spending Image Skill credits (hosted) — no local model execution path is provided
Supports a single input image per job; multi-image or multi-view inputs are not yet supported
Requires network access to api.image-skill.com; offline operation is not possible

use_cases.title: Product Photography to 3D Prototype
use_cases.description: A product designer has a single product photo and needs a quick 3D mesh for an internal mockup or stakeholder review without setting up Blender or a local GPU pipeline.

use_cases.title: Agent-Pipeline Asset Generation
use_cases.description: An AI agent workflow needs to generate a durable 3D asset URL from an image as part of a larger content pipeline, with cost controls and JSON recovery.
use_cases.target_user: AI agent developers and automation engineers

use_cases.title: Concept Art to Game Asset
use_cases.description: A game developer or 3D artist wants to quickly turn concept art into a starting mesh that can be imported into Unity, Unreal, or Blender for further refinement.

prompt_templates.title: Beginner: Run the Free Guide First
prompt_templates.scenario: First-time user who wants to understand what inputs are needed before spending credits
prompt_templates.prompt: Use the image-to-3d skill. Run the guide command first (with --guide) to discover what inputs the fal.trellis-image-to-3d model needs. Do not run the create or edit command yet. Report back what data.next_command_missing_inputs contains.

prompt_templates.title: Intermediate: Convert a Product Photo
prompt_templates.scenario: User has a product photo ready and wants to generate a glb mesh
prompt_templates.prompt: Use the image-to-3d skill. I have an input image uploaded to Image Skill with the ID image_abc123. Run the edit command with --model fal.trellis-image-to-3d, set --max-estimated-usd-per-image 0.04, and return the full JSON output including the hosted asset URL and cost receipt.

prompt_templates.title: Advanced: Agent Pipeline Integration
prompt_templates.scenario: Developer integrating 3D generation into an automated agent workflow with cost and recovery controls
prompt_templates.prompt: Use the image-to-3d skill inside my agent pipeline. For each image ID in the input queue, call `npx -y image-skill@latest edit` with --model fal.trellis-image-to-3d, --max-estimated-usd-per-image 0.04, and --json. Parse the JSON response, store data.asset_url and data.cost_receipt in my job ledger, and if the response includes data.next_command run that follow-up command.

prompt_templates.title: Advanced: Cost-Controlled Batch with Fallback
prompt_templates.scenario: User running multiple conversions who needs to cap total spend and report missing capabilities
prompt_templates.prompt: Use the image-to-3d skill. Process these 5 image IDs in sequence: [image_1, image_2, ...]. For each, set --max-estimated-usd-per-image 0.03 and use --json. If the API returns a missing-capability error, run `image-skill feedback create --json` with the attempted command and the error. Stop processing if cumulative spend exceeds $0.15 and report the partial results.

output_examples.input: Run the guide command to discover required inputs for the fal.trellis-image-to-3d model.
output: Missing inputs: one source image to convert (must be already uploaded as an Image Skill asset).
Next command copy is runnable: true.

output_examples.input: Execute the edit command for image_abc123 and return the hosted asset URL.

best_practices: Always run the --guide command first to verify inputs and cost before executing the create or edit command, avoiding wasted credit spend.
Always set --max-estimated-usd-per-image to enforce a per-image cost ceiling, especially in automated or batch workflows.
Use --json output in agent pipelines so the response can be parsed for asset URLs, job IDs, and cost receipts.
anti_patterns: Do not run the edit command without first running the guide command, as this can waste credits on incomplete input sets.
Do not omit --max-estimated-usd-per-image in batch workflows, since unexpected model costs could exceed your budget.
Do not pipe raw text output through shell parsing — always use --json for reliable machine consumption of results.

faq.question: What input does the image-to-3d skill require?
faq.answer: A single source image that has already been uploaded to Image Skill. The guide command will confirm any missing inputs before you spend credits.

faq.question: What format is the output 3D model?
faq.answer: The skill returns a hosted glb (binary glTF) mesh URL. The mesh includes texture data and can be imported into Unity, Unreal, Blender, or any glb-compatible viewer.

faq.question: Do I need my own provider API key or account?
faq.answer: No. The skill uses Image Skill's hosted runtime, so you do not need a fal.ai key, an OAuth flow, or a local model server. An optional IMAGE_SKILL_TOKEN can be used for restricted access.

faq.question: How much does each conversion cost?
faq.answer: Cost depends on the model. For fal.trellis-image-to-3d, a typical conversion costs between $0.03 and $0.04 per image. You can cap this with the --max-estimated-usd-per-image flag.

faq.question: Is the output asset durable and reusable?
faq.answer: Yes. The skill returns a durable hosted URL that future agents and sessions can reference. Job IDs, receipts, and recovery data are also returned in the JSON output.

faq.question: Can I use this skill in Claude Code or Codex?
faq.answer: Yes. The skill is supported in Claude, Codex, and Claude Code. Install it via the skills.sh adapter using the provided install command.
